channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: OpenAI locks GPT-5.5-Cyber behind velvet rope despite slamming Anthropic for doing exactly that
item.description: OpenAI is preparing a limited release of its GPT-5.5-Cyber model for select 'cyber defenders,' despite previously criticizing Anthropic for similar practices. This move highlights the competitive landscape in AI development and the ongoing debate over accessibility.

item.title: My home Wi-Fi was full of dead zones - here are 6 solutions that actually worked
item.description: After struggling with Wi-Fi dead zones, the author shares six effective solutions that improved connectivity throughout their home. This practical advice can help others facing similar issues.

item.title: SpaceX rocket set for unintentional Moon landing – well, a piece of it anyway
item.description: A piece of SpaceX's Falcon 9 upper stage is expected to unintentionally land on the Moon, traveling at high speeds. This event underscores the increasing amount of space debris and its unpredictable impacts.

item.title: Pro-Iran crew turns DDoS into shakedown as Ubuntu.com stays down
item.description: Canonical's Ubuntu.com is under a DDoS attack orchestrated by a pro-Iran hacktivist group, which is demanding payment to cease the attack. This incident raises concerns about cybersecurity threats facing open-source platforms.

item.title: Best AirPods of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: An expert reviews the best AirPods available in 2026, providing insights based on extensive testing of Apple's headphone lineup. This guide helps consumers make informed purchasing decisions.

item.title: UK pensions dept goes shopping for spy-van tech with £2M surveillance tender
item.description: The UK's Department for Work and Pensions is seeking to enhance surveillance capabilities with a £2 million tender for covert cameras and live-streaming technology to combat fraud. This reflects a growing trend in government surveillance.

item.title: I ditched my laptop for XR headsets, tablets, and phones - and learned an important lesson
item.description: The author shares their experience of replacing a laptop with XR headsets, tablets, and phones for content creation, discovering new ways to enhance creativity. This shift illustrates the evolving landscape of digital tools.

item.title: Moving from AI pilots to business-wide value requires a superhighway - how to ramp up
item.description: Accenture's research emphasizes the need for companies to achieve early wins with AI investments to transition from isolated AI projects to broader business value. This approach is crucial for sustainable AI integration.

item.title: Who needs ghost train scares when Windows is such a fright?
item.description: An exploration of common tech issues that can cause frustration among IT professionals, likening them to horror stories. This light-hearted take reflects the challenges of working with technology.

item.title: Inexpensive seafloor-hopping submersibles could stoke deep-sea science—and mining
item.description: NOAA's research vessel Rainier is mapping the Pacific seafloor to identify critical mineral deposits, utilizing inexpensive submersibles to enhance deep-sea exploration. This initiative could advance both science and mining efforts.

item.title: Passport to £££: Home Office adds £216M to travel doc contract before a single bid's been placed
item.description: The UK's Home Office has increased its passport production contract to £576 million, delaying the start date by a year and raising annual costs by a third. This change comes as the UK issues eight million passports annually, indicating a significant investment in travel documentation.

item.title: 4 interior designers told us what smart tech you should actually buy
item.description: Interior designers recommend smart home products that combine aesthetics with functionality, emphasizing the importance of choosing tech that enhances home design. Their insights aim to guide consumers in making informed purchases.

item.title: Forget productivity: Here are 5 strategic shifts that drive real AI value
item.description: Instead of merely focusing on productivity, experts suggest strategic shifts that can unlock real value from AI tools. This approach emphasizes the need for organizations to rethink their AI strategies for meaningful outcomes.

item.title: Trump’s mass firing just dealt another blow to American science
item.description: The mass firing of the National Science Foundation's board, which oversees $9 billion in research funding, poses a significant threat to scientific progress in the US. This event raises concerns about the future of federal support for scientific research.

item.title: A new US phone network for Christians aims to block porn and gender-related content
item.description: A new US cell phone network aimed at Christians will block porn and gender-related content at the network level, marking a significant shift in mobile service offerings. This initiative raises questions about censorship and user control.

item.title: DVLA's 14-week driving license fiasco – the tech, people and chatbot trying to clear it
item.description: The DVLA is facing a backlog in driving license applications due to medical checks, with processing times exceeding 14 weeks. New technology and a chatbot have been introduced to help clear the backlog.

item.title: Microsoft wants lawyers to trust its new AI agent in Word documents
item.description: Microsoft is introducing a specialized AI agent in Word for legal teams, designed to streamline document management and negotiation processes. This tool aims to enhance trust and efficiency in legal workflows.

item.title: India in talks with US, Anthropic for Mythos access; no Indian firms in Project Glasswing yet
item.description: India is negotiating with the US and Anthropic for access to advanced AI technologies while facing compliance issues. This highlights the country's efforts to bolster its cybersecurity capabilities amid emerging AI threats.

item.title: The Secret Weapon Against AI Dominance
item.description: The future of creative labor hinges on the ability to copyright AI-generated work, raising significant legal and ethical questions. This issue is crucial for artists and industries impacted by AI advancements.

item.title: SPRIND opens applications for €125M competition to build Europe’s first frontier AI labs
item.description: SPRIND has launched a €125 million competition to establish Europe's first frontier AI labs, encouraging applicants to innovate beyond current AI models. This initiative aims to foster groundbreaking advancements in AI technology.

item.title: autoclaude-cli 2.5.1
item.description: The release of autoclaude-cli 2.5.1 allows users to run orchestration plans locally with their Claude Max/Pro subscription, enhancing user control and flexibility.

item.title: hf 1.13.0
item.description: The Hugging Face Hub has released version 1.13.0 of its CLI, enabling easier interaction with the platform for developers and researchers.

item.title: Tech sector is cutting jobs but this time it feels much different
item.description: The tech sector is experiencing job cuts despite high profits, as AI is both consuming investment and displacing workers, indicating a shift in the industry landscape.

item.title: torchlens 2.16.0
item.description: Torchlens 2.16.0 has been released, allowing users to extract activations from PyTorch models and save them in portable formats, enhancing model usability.

item.title: WATCH: Artemis II crew discusses historic mission on 'GMA'
item.description: The Artemis II crew discussed their historic mission around the moon on 'GMA', marking a significant milestone in human space exploration.

item.title: CVE MCP Server Turns Claude Into a Fully Capable Security Analyst With 27 Tools Across 21 APIs
item.description: The CVE MCP Server project transforms Claude AI into a security analyst by integrating it with 27 intelligence tools, enhancing vulnerability management for security teams.

item.title: WATCH: Never-before-seen views from the Sagrada Familia
item.description: Michael Strahan shared exclusive views from the Sagrada Familia on 'GMA', highlighting the architectural marvel's long construction history.

item.title: aiui-mcp 0.4.25
item.description: The aiui-mcp 0.4.25 release introduces a server for native macOS dialogs in Claude Code sessions, enhancing user experience for developers.

item.title: KIEFER AI Open Hackathon 2026: Seven teams, global open-source launch, and Greece’s first sovereign AI infrastructure
item.description: The KIEFER AI Open Hackathon 2026 aims to launch Greece’s first sovereign AI infrastructure, fostering international collaboration and innovation in AI.

item.title: The next Galaxy Book could be a flagship Aluminium OS laptop powered by One UI 9
item.description: Samsung is reportedly developing a new Galaxy Book laptop series powered by Aluminium OS and featuring Galaxy AI, indicating a shift towards integrated AI solutions.

item.title: jupyterlab-claude-code-extension 1.0.29
item.description: The JupyterLab Claude Code extension 1.0.29 allows users to manage Claude Code CLI sessions easily from a side panel, enhancing workflow efficiency.

item.title: How Good Is Google’s Gemini AI at Making Travel Plans? - The New York Times
item.description: A New York Times article evaluates Google's Gemini AI's capabilities in planning travel, highlighting its potential and limitations in real-world applications.

item.title: pycastle 0.1.1.7
item.description: The pycastle 0.1.1.7 release introduces a Python orchestrator designed for managing autonomous Claude Code agents within Docker environments.

item.title: Android Auto doesn’t let you dismiss alarms, but Google is working on a solution
item.description: Google is reportedly working on a solution to allow users to dismiss alarms in Android Auto, addressing a long-standing user complaint.

item.title: Thursday: Hili dialogue
item.description: An article discusses the significance of April 30, 2026, marking National Bugs Bunny Day, but lacks relevance to AI or tech.

item.title: Managing the Machines: How AI Intensifies Work
item.description: The article explores how AI, while intended to boost productivity, often complicates work processes, leading to increased workloads.

item.title: agent-recall-ai added to PyPI
item.description: The agent-recall-ai tool added to PyPI offers structured session checkpointing for AI agents, helping them manage context limits and session interruptions.

item.title: HPE expands ProLiant edge network portfolio
item.description: HPE has expanded its ProLiant edge network portfolio with new chassis and servers optimized for use in harsh environments, targeting various industries.

item.title: China launches months-long campaign against AI misuse targeting deepfakes, fraud, and disinformation
item.description: China has launched a campaign against AI misuse, focusing on deepfakes and fraud, amid allegations of large-scale AI theft by the U.S.

item.title: AI Code Review Only Catches Half of Your Bugs
item.description: An article reveals that AI code review tools only catch about half of coding bugs, emphasizing the need for human oversight in AI-driven development.

item.title: residual added to PyPI
item.description: A new implementation of Residual Networks (ResNet) has been added to PyPI, enhancing PyTorch's capabilities for deep learning. This addition allows developers to utilize a more configurable version of ResNet for their projects.

item.title: Who Trusts the Money Queen?
item.description: Amanda Frances, known for helping women manifest their dreams, faces scrutiny from former clients who feel misled about her financial success. This raises questions about the ethics of her business practices.

item.title: Stop asking AI to read your mind. Use this prompt to get better answers
item.description: Experts advise against vague prompts when using AI tools like ChatGPT, suggesting that clearer, more specific instructions yield better results. This guidance aims to improve user interactions with AI.

item.title: Your Horoscope for the Full Flower Moon in Scorpio
item.description: The Full Flower Moon in Scorpio will occur on May 1, presenting as a micromoon. Astrological insights are provided for this lunar event.

item.title: YouTube Premium is quietly winning the war against ad blockers
item.description: YouTube has seen a rise in Premium subscribers, attributed to its ongoing efforts against ad blockers. This trend indicates a shift in user preferences towards ad-free experiences.

item.title: hf 1.13.0rc0
item.description: A new command-line interface (CLI) has been extracted from the huggingface_hub library, facilitating interaction with the Hugging Face Hub. This tool aims to streamline AI model management.

item.title: Italy’s antitrust authority closes probes into DeepSeek, Mistral, and Nova AI over AI hallucination disclosures
item.description: Italy's antitrust authority has closed investigations into DeepSeek, Mistral, and Nova AI after they agreed to transparency commitments regarding AI hallucinations. This sets a standard for AI accountability in the industry.

item.title: litert-torch-nightly 0.10.0.dev20260430
item.description: The LiteRT library has introduced support for PyTorch model conversion, enhancing the efficiency of deploying models. This update is aimed at improving performance in AI applications.

item.title: llama-index-tools-swiss-truth 0.1.0
item.description: LlamaIndex has released tools for Swiss Truth, a certified knowledge base designed for AI agents. This aims to improve the reliability of AI interactions.

item.title: openagentd added to PyPI
item.description: The openagentd package has been added to PyPI, enabling self-hosted AI agents with features like streaming chat and persistent memory. This development supports more complex AI interactions.
